message:
fix(generators): ladder_graph / circular_ladder_graph handle degenerate n<3 (br-uwa6w)

Review pass found fnx.ladder_graph(0) raises NetworkXError('n must
be >= 1') where upstream nx returns an empty Graph; similarly
fnx.circular_ladder_graph(n) for n in {0, 1, 2} raises 'n must be
>= 3' where upstream returns degenerate-but-valid graphs. The rust
primitives have minimum-n guards; the Python wrappers now dispatch
to the pure-Python fallback path (which uses ``empty_graph`` +
explicit add_edges_from) when n is below the rust threshold.

Regression tests parametrized over n ∈ {0, 1, 2, 3, 5} compare
node and edge sets against nx for both generators.

Suite: 3967 passed (was 3957) / 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -857,6 +857,7 @@
 {"id":"franken_networkx-uphdr","title":"17 more add_edge(u, v, key=key, **attrs) collision sites beyond the ones fixed in 9b5ba5f","description":"``franken_networkx-9x7r0`` fixed 5 call sites (+1 readwrite mirror)
of the ``add_edge(u, v, key=key, **dict(attrs))`` pattern that
collides when an edge attribute is literally named ``'key'``. A
follow-up grep shows the same pattern still exists in **17 other
locations** that are all user-reachable through standard API calls:

Production code (15 sites):

    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:1214  _nan_filtered_graph
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:1445  _networkx_graph_for_traversal_parity (helper used by bfs/dfs family)
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:6401  (product-graph constructor; L-shift)
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:6410  (product-graph constructor; R-shift)
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:7063  (subgraph/induced builder)
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:7273  compose / compose_all
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:7315  compose_all with rename
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:12899 product graph (tensor_product / etc)
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:12909 product graph continuation
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:15104 _DirectedReverseView.copy
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:15122 _DirectedReverseView.copy (multi branch)
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:20149 graph assembly
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:22796 relabel_nodes / copy w/ map
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:22983 ``_copy_with_component_relabeling``-style
    python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:23104 final graph assembly

Support modules (2 sites):

    python/franken_networkx/backend.py:411            _fnx_to_nx multigraph edge copy
    python/franken_networkx/readwrite/__init__.py:621 gexf-style reader shim

Reproducer template (adjust per site — most go through ``compose``,
``cartesian_product``, ``reverse``, ``relabel_nodes``, or
``backend._fnx_to_nx``)::

    import franken_networkx as fnx
    a = fnx.MultiGraph(); a.add_edge('x', 'y', weight=1)
    a['x']['y'][0]['key'] = 'sentinel'
    b = fnx.MultiGraph(); b.add_edge('p', 'q')
    # Any of these will TypeError on 'key' collision:
    fnx.compose(a, b)
    fnx.cartesian_product(a, b)
    fnx.reverse(fnx.MultiDiGraph(a))     # flip to digraph first
    fnx.relabel_nodes(a, {'x': 'X'}, copy=True)

Fix template (same as 9x7r0): replace::

    for u, v, key, attrs in H.edges(keys=True, data=True):
        target.add_edge(u, v, key=key, **dict(attrs))

with::

    target.add_edges_from(
        (u, v, key, dict(attrs))
        for u, v, key, attrs in H.edges(keys=True, data=True)
    )

For sites that do per-edge NaN filtering or node-relabeling inside
the loop body, keep the loop but switch to::

    target.add_edges_from([(u, v, key, dict(attrs))])  # single-tuple add

or build a list and bulk-add outside.

Regression test: extend
``test_attribute_access_parity.py::test_multigraph_copy_preserves_edge_attribute_named_key``
to cover compose / cartesian_product / reverse / relabel_nodes. Each
should round-trip an edge with attr ``'key'`` without TypeError.

Scope note: only production Python sites are listed. ``backend.py``
site is user-facing (via the nx-backend dispatch path). The
readwrite site only fires on a specific reader shim.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:02:54.670977743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:35:14.582930Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:35:14.582930Z","close_reason":"Fixed in c2dda76: 12 sites auto-rewritten via regex; 5 tuple-node sites and 2 edge_key sites fixed by hand. Regression tests for cartesian_product, relabel_nodes, tensor_product added. Full suite 3898 passed."}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-uqcd","title":"Readwrite: gml_declares_directed scans full graph","description":"Detect directed flag even when declared after node/edge blocks; avoid false undirected selection in Python read_gml.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T17:28:36.409890293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T17:28:38.617222939Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T17:28:38.616956044Z","close_reason":"Scan graph-level tokens for directed flag and add regression test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-uu5t","title":"[A4] Commit or revert the 10 in-flight test files","description":"**Track:** A. **Priority:** P0. **Type:** task.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:23:21.753437351Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:24:15.983127553Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T00:24:15.982931732Z","close_reason":"duplicate from dry-run double-create","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_networkx-uwa6w","title":"REVIEW: [LOW] ladder_graph(0) / circular_ladder_graph(0) raise where upstream returns empty/2-node graph","description":"Review pass found a generator edge-case parity gap.

``fnx.ladder_graph(0)`` raises ``NetworkXError('n must be >= 1')``;
upstream ``nx.ladder_graph(0)`` returns an empty Graph (0 nodes, 0
edges). Similarly ``fnx.circular_ladder_graph(0)`` raises where
upstream returns a 2-node graph with one edge (the degenerate
``C_2``).

Reproducer::

    import franken_networkx as fnx, networkx as nx

    nx.ladder_graph(0).number_of_nodes()          # -> 0
    fnx.ladder_graph(0)                            # NetworkXError

    nx.circular_ladder_graph(0).number_of_nodes() # -> 2
    fnx.circular_ladder_graph(0)                   # NetworkXError

Location: ``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py`` — the public
wrappers call ``_rust_ladder_graph(n)`` / ``_rust_circular_ladder_graph(n)``;
the rust side rejects n=0.

Impact: [LOW] — unusual input, but parity-contract divergence. Any
code that iterates ``ladder_graph(n)`` for n in range(k) and expects
nx semantics gets a crash at the start of the loop on fnx.

Fix sketch: handle n=0 at the Python layer before dispatching::

    def ladder_graph(n, create_using=None):
        if n == 0:
            return empty_graph(0, create_using)
        if create_using is None:
            return _rust_ladder_graph(n)
        # ... existing fallback path

    def circular_ladder_graph(n, create_using=None):
        if n == 0:
            g = empty_graph(0, create_using)
            # nx returns a K_2 here; the 2-node/1-edge result
            g.add_edge(0, 1)  # verify against nx — may be slightly different
            return g
        ...

Also worth aligning error classes for negative n (fnx raises
OverflowError; nx raises NetworkXError). Separate minor issue.

Tag: [LOW].","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:40:57.031192813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:41:04.984717050Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-uws","title":"clustering() on DiGraph uses undirected formula instead of directed clustering","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:12:54.538005432Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:18:01.481834356Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T20:18:01.481572220Z","close_reason":"Implemented directed clustering coefficient (Fagiolo 2007) in Rust. Uses chain(preds,succs) iteration matching NX's directed triangle counting. Python binding now dispatches to directed version for DiGraphs. Exact match with NX on complete digraph K4, DAG, cycle, and complex test cases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-ux5o","title":"parity: predecessor should preserve source iteration order","description":"predecessor(source=...) currently returns the right predecessor mapping but in a different insertion order than NetworkX. On a simple path a-b-c-d, FrankenNetworkX yields {'d': ['c'], 'c': ['b'], 'b': ['a'], 'a': []} while NetworkX yields {'a': [], 'b': ['a'], 'c': ['b'], 'd': ['c']}. Preserve NetworkX's observable mapping order, including cutoff queries.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:08:32.277055305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:35:06.805515667Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:35:06.803671365Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-uxby","title":"Add planar_layout transformed-graph metamorphic coverage","description":"Add metamorphic tests proving planar_layout matches NetworkX for transformed planar graphs with self-loops and tuple node labels, including store_pos_as behavior where applicable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T12:32:10.203662291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T12:33:16.438050747Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T12:33:16.436079235Z","close_reason":"Completed planar_layout transformed-graph metamorphic coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -9130,8 +9130,14 @@ def bull_graph(create_using=None):
 
 
 def circular_ladder_graph(n, create_using=None):
-    """Return the circular ladder graph."""
-    if create_using is None:
+    """Return the circular ladder graph.
+
+    Degenerate cases (``n < 3``) use the pure-Python fallback path
+    — the rust primitive rejects those with ``n must be >= 3``,
+    but upstream nx returns degenerate-but-valid graphs for
+    n ∈ {0, 1, 2} (franken_networkx-uwa6w).
+    """
+    if create_using is None and n >= 3:
         return _rust_circular_ladder_graph(n)
 
     G = ladder_graph(n, create_using)
@@ -9141,8 +9147,13 @@ def circular_ladder_graph(n, create_using=None):
 
 
 def ladder_graph(n, create_using=None):
-    """Return the ladder graph."""
-    if create_using is None:
+    """Return the ladder graph.
+
+    Degenerate case ``n == 0`` is an empty graph (matches upstream
+    nx). The rust primitive raises on n=0, so special-case it
+    (franken_networkx-uwa6w).
+    """
+    if create_using is None and n > 0:
         return _rust_ladder_graph(n)
 
     G = empty_graph(2 * n, create_using)

@@ -151,3 +151,28 @@ def norm(graph):
     r_fnx = getattr(fnx, op_name)(build(fnx), build_h(fnx))
     r_nx = getattr(nx, op_name)(build(nx), build_h(nx))
     assert norm(r_fnx) == norm(r_nx)
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# franken_networkx-uwa6w: ladder_graph / circular_ladder_graph edge cases
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("n", [0, 1, 2, 3, 5])
+def test_ladder_graph_matches_networkx_for_small_n(n):
+    fg = fnx.ladder_graph(n)
+    ng = nx.ladder_graph(n)
+    assert set(fg.nodes()) == set(ng.nodes())
+    assert set(frozenset(e) for e in fg.edges()) == set(
+        frozenset(e) for e in ng.edges()
+    )
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("n", [0, 1, 2, 3, 5])
+def test_circular_ladder_graph_matches_networkx_for_small_n(n):
+    fg = fnx.circular_ladder_graph(n)
+    ng = nx.circular_ladder_graph(n)
+    assert set(fg.nodes()) == set(ng.nodes())
+    assert set(frozenset(e) for e in fg.edges()) == set(
+        frozenset(e) for e in ng.edges()
+    )
